---
title: "LRPGF01b: Factors associated with gains in word reading (W) — treated-only companion"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF01b is the **treated-only** companion to LRPGF01 ("gains while on
intervention"): the same gain-factors model for word reading (`ewrswr`) restricted
to on-intervention period rows (it excludes the waitlist arm's untreated period 1).

_No treatment contrast — this is a factor-association model._ Once restricted to on-intervention rows, the on-intervention indicator is constant,
so the treatment term and its interactions are **dropped**: there is no randomised
contrast here. This model reports the **adjusted associations** of the remaining
factors (own baseline, age, cognitive ability, skills TR/TE/R/E/L/N/B, age $\times$ ability)
with word-reading gains *among the treated*. Under the revised DAG
(`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, revised 2026-07-10) every coefficient is an
adjusted association: the child random intercept is a partial, shrunken stand-in for
between-child heterogeneity and does **not** control latent general ability, so these
internal slopes remain descriptive associations — read as "associated with", never
"drives". SES is excluded
(non-DAG / redundant). Compare these associations with LRPGF01.

## Model

$$
\operatorname{logit}\mu_{i,p} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}[p]}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(W_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + \gamma_{\text{ability}}\,z(\text{blocks})
  + \sum_{s\in\{TR,TE,R,E,L,N,B\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_{\text{int}}\,(\text{age}\times\text{ability})
  + u_{\text{child}[i]},
$$

on the on-intervention rows only, with
$W_{\text{post}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$,
$N = 79$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
